landing.heroTitle: Create a business website in 30 minutes, no developer needed
landing.heroSubtitle: You get a complete website with everything you need: design, copy, contact form, and online payments.
landing.examplesSubtitle: Suitable for different businesses, restaurants, salons, shops, and more.
landing.exampleRestaurantDesc: Menu, bookings, and contact details so customers can find you easily.
landing.exampleSalonDesc: Services, time slots, and booking without phone call confusion.
landing.exampleShopDesc: Products and card payments so you can sell directly online.
landing.how1Desc: Tell us what you do in plain language with no technical terms.
landing.how2Desc: We automatically build the website based on your description.
landing.how3Title: You get a link and go live
landing.how3Desc: Share the address and start getting customers and payments.
landing.ctaBandSubtitle: The first two website changes are free. After that, activate the €20/month plan for unlimited changes.
landing.footer: © {{year}} Web Work. Complete solution for creating and managing your business website.
landing.audienceSubtitle: Web Work is built for small business owners who want a professional website without technical headaches.
landing.audience1Desc: Restaurants, salons, shops, studios, craftsmen, and local services that want their first online presence.
landing.audience2Desc: Photographers, coaches, consultants, and other professionals who need a portfolio site with a contact form.
landing.audience3Desc: When you're launching an idea and need to quickly present your service, collect inquiries and payments.
landing.faqSubtitle: Common questions about building, hosting, and maintaining a site with Web Work.
landing.faq1Q: How long does it take to build my site?
landing.faq1A: Most sites are generated in under an hour. Typically within a day you can describe your business, review the result, and publish your site online.
landing.faq2Q: Do I need to know how to code or design?
landing.faq2A: No. You describe your business in plain language and Web Work prepares the full website — design, structure, and content.
landing.faq3Q: Can I use my own domain?
landing.faq3A: Yes. You can connect your own domain through DNS settings. We document every step in the docs.
landing.faq4A: Hosting is €20/month and includes a secure HTTPS certificate, global CDN, regular backups, and 24/7 infrastructure support.
landing.faq5A: The integration is with Stripe — you accept debit and credit card payments directly on your site, with no extra setup on your side.
landing.faq6Q: Can I make changes after the site is built?
landing.faq6A: Yes. You make changes through an AI chat — describe what you want and the site updates automatically. The first changes are free.
landing.faq7A: Yes. All communication, documentation, and technical support is available in Bulgarian.

connectDomain.customPanelSubtitle: Use this only for a domain you bought from a registrar, for example www.yourbrand.com.
connectDomain.helpTooltip: How to buy a domain and edit DNS
connectDomain.firstPartyNotice: You already have a working address on our domain, and it does not need DNS changes. Use this section only if you want to replace it with your own domain.
connectDomain.hostingEnvWarn: This server does not have HOSTING_SITES_HOST configured. You can verify ownership with TXT only; add the variable and redeploy for full CNAME verification.
connectDomain.httpsHint: HTTPS for your hostname still requires an edge certificate, for example Let's Encrypt or Cloudflare in front of this server. The CNAME target {{target}} may open over HTTP for testing until TLS is configured.
connectDomain.customHostnameHint: Example: www.yourbrand.com or yourbrand.com. Both work — for an apex domain, Cloudflare handles it automatically via CNAME flattening.
connectDomain.stepsLine1: 1. Enter the exact address visitors will open, e.g. www.yourbrand.com or yourbrand.com.
connectDomain.stepsLine2: 2. Click "Show DNS records" so we can generate the TXT and CNAME records for that address.
connectDomain.stepsLine3: 3. Add those records at your registrar, then click "Verify DNS".
connectDomain.cloudflareNoteBody: Turn the orange cloud OFF for the CNAME record so it reads "DNS only" (grey cloud). A proxied CNAME hides the chain from public DNS and blocks our certificate from being issued, so the domain never goes live. For an apex domain (@) add the same CNAME — Cloudflare flattens it to A records for you.
connectDomain.txtHelp: TXT — prove that you control the domain
connectDomain.panelSubtitle: Point a name purchased from a registrar to this hosted app.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.s1Body: Web Work (fornaxelit.com) is an AI-powered platform that lets you create, host, and manage a business website without writing code. By creating an account or using the service you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use Web Work.
terms.s2Body: You must be at least 16 years old (or the age of digital consent in your jurisdiction, whichever is higher) to create an account. By registering you confirm that you meet this requirement.
terms.s3Body:
You register with an email address and password. You are responsible for keeping your credentials secure.

  • Passwords must be at least 8 characters.
  • Email changes and password changes require a 6-digit verification code sent to your email.
  • You may delete your account at any time from Settings. You must remove hosted projects first.
terms.s4Body:
Web Work offers the following paid services, billed in EUR through Stripe:

  • Site generation — one-time payment of €150. Includes the AI-generated website, source code download, 1 month of free hosting, and bonus improvement credits.
  • Hosting — €20/month recurring subscription. Keeps your site online with a public address or custom domain. If the subscription is not activated after the free month, the site is taken offline automatically.
  • Improvement credits — €1.50 per credit (pack of 20 for €20). Each credit allows one AI-powered change to your site.

All prices include VAT where applicable. You can manage or cancel subscriptions via the Stripe customer portal at any time.
terms.s5Body:
Websites are generated using third-party AI models (Anthropic Claude and OpenAI). You should review the generated content before publishing.

  • AI output may occasionally be inaccurate or inappropriate. You are responsible for reviewing and editing your site content.
  • Web Work does not guarantee that the generated code will be error-free or fit any particular purpose.
  • You must not use Web Work to generate content that is illegal, harmful, defamatory, or infringes third-party rights.
terms.s6Body:
Your content: You own the text, images, product listings, blog posts, and other content you add to your site.

Generated code: Once you pay for a project, you receive full rights to download, modify, and use the generated source code for your business purposes.

Our platform: The Web Work platform, branding, and underlying technology remain our property.
terms.s7Body:
We strive for high availability but do not guarantee 100% uptime. Scheduled maintenance, infrastructure incidents, or abuse may cause temporary interruptions.

  • Hosting is provided on our infrastructure at fornaxelit.com.
  • You may connect a custom domain by configuring DNS records as described in the documentation.
  • If your hosting subscription lapses or is cancelled, your site will be taken offline. Your project files remain available for download as long as your account exists.
terms.s8Body:
Web Work can send transactional emails on behalf of your site (e.g. order confirmations, booking notifications) through Resend.

  • You may configure a custom sender domain with DNS verification.
  • You must not use the email feature for spam, bulk marketing, or unsolicited messages.
  • We may suspend email sending if we detect abuse or high bounce/complaint rates.
terms.s9Body:
• Do not use Web Work for illegal activities, fraud, phishing, or distributing malware.
  • Do not attempt to access other users' accounts or data.
  • Do not reverse-engineer, scrape, or overload the platform infrastructure.
  • We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.
terms.s10Body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Web Work and its operators are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from the use of the service, including but not limited to lost profits, data loss, or business interruption. Our total liability is limited to the amount you have paid us in the 12 months preceding the claim.
terms.s11Body: We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes will be communicated by email or a notice on the platform. Continued use of Web Work after such changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated Terms.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Body: Web Work (fornaxelit.com) is an AI-powered website builder. This policy explains what personal data we collect, why, and how we protect it.
privacy.s2Body:
Account data: email address and a securely hashed password (bcrypt). We also store the date you registered.

Payment data: payments are processed by Stripe. We store your Stripe customer ID but never see or store your card number. Please see Stripe's Privacy Policy.

Project data: the website content and source files you create through Web Work, including text, images you upload, product listings, blog posts, and configuration.

Chat messages: your conversations with the AI planning assistant are stored so you can return to them.

Email data: if you use the transactional email feature, we store sender configuration, templates, and delivery logs (recipient address, status, timestamps).
privacy.s3Body:
When visitors browse a site hosted on Web Work, we collect:

  • Page path and referrer URL
  • Device type (desktop, mobile, tablet) derived from the user agent string
  • A daily visitor identifier — a truncated SHA-256 hash of the visitor's IP address combined with the project ID and the current date. The raw IP address is never stored.

This data helps site owners understand traffic patterns. We do not use it for advertising or cross-site tracking.

No cookies: Web Work does not set cookies. Authentication uses a JWT token stored in your browser's local storage.
privacy.s4Body:
• Stripe — payment processing (privacy policy)
  • Anthropic (Claude) and OpenAI — AI generation. Your planning conversation and project description are sent to these providers to generate your website. See Anthropic's and OpenAI's privacy policies.
  • Resend — transactional email delivery (privacy policy)
  • Cloudflare — DNS and TLS certificates for custom domains
privacy.s5Body:
• To create and maintain your account
  • To generate, build, and host your website
  • To process payments and manage subscriptions
  • To send transactional emails (verification codes, order notifications) to you and your customers
  • To provide analytics about visitors to your hosted site
  • To detect and prevent abuse of the platform

We do not sell your data or use it for advertising.
privacy.s6Body:
Your account data and project files are retained for as long as your account is active.

  • You can delete your account from Settings. This permanently removes your user record.
  • You must remove hosted projects before deleting your account. Project source files are deleted when the project is deleted.
  • Analytics page views and email logs associated with a deleted project are removed automatically (cascade delete).
  • Stripe retains its own payment records independently according to their retention policies.
privacy.s7Body: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt. Sensitive environment variables (e.g. connected Stripe keys for your project) are encrypted with AES-256-GCM at rest. All traffic is served over HTTPS. We follow industry-standard practices, but no system can guarantee absolute security.
privacy.s8Body: If you are in the European Economic Area, you have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data, restrict or object to processing, and data portability. To exercise these rights, contact us at the email below. We will respond within 30 days.
privacy.s9Body: Web Work is not directed at children under 16. We do not knowingly collect data from anyone under 16. If you believe a child has created an account, please contact us so we can delete it.
privacy.s10Body: For privacy questions or data requests, email us at support@webwork.bg.

previewFrame.errorNotYetHint: The preview server may still be starting or the project is not ready. Try “Try again” in a minute or go back to chat and start a build.
previewFrame.errorCouldNotStartHint: Files are missing or the preview service needs a restart. For Docker: docker compose restart app-runner
previewFrame.errorHttpHint: Use refresh in the toolbar or “Try again” below. If it continues, rebuild from chat.

theme.uploadTooltip: Upload a logo or photo — we will extract the colors automatically
theme.fromImageHint: Upload an image — we will extract the palette automatically
theme.extractError: Failed to extract colors. Try a different image.

domainDocs.title: Domains and your online site
domainDocs.intro: Web Work hosts your app. You can use a custom domain (e.g. www.yourbrand.com) by purchasing it from a registrar and pointing it to the address we show on the preview screen. This guide explains both steps in plain language.
domainDocs.alertNoSell: Web Work does not sell domains. You buy a domain from a registrar (an accredited company), then connect it here.
domainDocs.glossaryDomain: Domain — the address people type in the browser, e.g. coffeehouse.com.
domainDocs.glossaryRegistrar: Registrar — the company where you search for, buy, and renew domains.
domainDocs.glossaryDns: DNS — the system that tells the internet where to send visitors (records like CNAME and TXT).
domainDocs.glossarySubdomain: Subdomain — a prefix, e.g. www in www.coffeehouse.com.
domainDocs.buyIntro: A one-time step per brand. The process is similar across most registrars.
domainDocs.whereBuyBody: Common options: Cloudflare Registrar, Namecheap, Porkbun, Google Domains (now Squarespace Domains), and others. Compare first-year and renewal prices; pick a company you are comfortable logging into when changing DNS.
domainDocs.typicalFlowL1: Create an account at the registrar.
domainDocs.typicalFlowL2: Search for the name you want (e.g. yourbrand + .com).
domainDocs.typicalFlowL3: Add to cart and pay. You rent the name annually (renewing keeps it yours).
domainDocs.typicalFlowL4: Confirm the email from the registrar to complete the order.
domainDocs.typicalFlowL5: Open the registrar panel and find DNS, DNS records, or Manage DNS — this is where you will add the records Web Work shows.
domainDocs.costsBody: The first year is often discounted; subsequent years cost more. Enable auto-renew if you do not want to lose the name when it expires. If payment fails, the domain may become available to others.
domainDocs.privacyBody: Many registrars offer free or paid WHOIS privacy so your personal details are not published. Enable it to reduce spam.
domainDocs.dnsTitle: 2. DNS basics (what you add at the registrar)
domainDocs.dnsAfterHosting: After you enable hosting in Web Work, the preview page shows two types of records:
domainDocs.cnameBody: A CNAME says “this hostname is an alias for another.” For example, www can point to the Web Work target (project ID + our hosting host). This routes traffic to the hosted app.
domainDocs.txtBody: A TXT record stores text. We add a short string to verify that you control the domain before we accept it as connected.
domainDocs.propBody: DNS changes are not instant everywhere. From a few minutes to up to 48 hours (rare). If verification fails, wait and try again. TTL on old records slows the update.
domainDocs.connectTitle: 3. Connecting a domain in Web Work
domainDocs.connectL1: Open the Preview page for the project (active hosting subscription required).
domainDocs.connectL2: In “Connect the domain” enter the exact hostname visitors will use (often www.yourbrand.com).
domainDocs.connectL3: Click “Save domain”, then copy the TXT and CNAME records to your registrar.
domainDocs.connectL4: Click “Verify DNS” after the records are saved. If it fails, check for typos.
domainDocs.apexWarn: Apex domains (yourbrand.com without www) work if your registrar supports CNAME flattening or ALIAS/ANAME records at the root. Cloudflare does this automatically. If your registrar does not support it, use www.yourbrand.com or redirect the apex to www.
domainDocs.httpsBody: Browsers expect encrypted HTTPS. After DNS points to Web Work hosting, the operator provides a certificate for the hostname (e.g. Let’s Encrypt or a load balancer). A certificate warning may mean propagation is still in progress or an edge setup is missing — check with your Web Work administrator.

support.title: Contact support
support.body: Describe the issue or question you are running into. Leave a contact email and phone so we can reach you directly.
support.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the problem, what you were trying to do, and anything we should know.
support.sentBody: Thanks — we received your ticket and will contact you using the email or phone you provided.
support.errorEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
support.errorPhoneInvalid: Please enter a valid phone number (at least 6 digits).
support.errorGeneric: Could not send your message. Please try again.
